data.title: Binance Alpha Airdrops 295 H Tokens to Eligible Users
data.description: Binance announced on X that Binance Alpha's second wave of Humanity Protocol (H) airdrop rewards is live.Users with at least 242 Binance Alpha Points can claim an airdrop of 295 H tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the rewards are not fully distributed,  the score threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes.Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha Points. Users must confirm their claim on the Alpha Events page within 24 hours; otherwise, it will be deemed that users have given up claiming the airdrop.

data.title: BNB Chain's Real-World Asset Value Doubles to $1.6 Billion
data.description: According to PANews, the value of real-world assets on the BNB Chain has doubled over the past 30 days, reaching $1.6 billion. This significant increase highlights the growing interest and investment in blockchain-based assets. The BNB Chain continues to attract attention as it expands its offerings and capabilities in the digital asset space.

data.title: Anthropic Eyes $300 Billion Valuation Amid IPO Plans
data.description: According to Odaily, artificial intelligence company Anthropic is in discussions for a new round of venture capital funding, with an anticipated valuation of $300 billion. The company has also engaged California law firm Wilson Sonsini for advisory services regarding its planned initial public offering (IPO), targeting a 2026 listing. Analysts suggest that the IPO could provide Anthropic with substantial capital, enabling it to compete with Sam Altman's OpenAI. Research by Deutsche Bank analysts Adrian Cox and Stefan Abrudan indicates some vulnerabilities in OpenAI's operations, noting a slowdown in consumer subscription growth for its ChatGPT service. Anthropic is perceived as having a greater potential for profitability compared to OpenAI.

data.title: Ethereum Surpasses $3,000 Amid Optimism for Upcoming Upgrade
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Ethereum has regained the $3,000 mark, driven by market optimism surrounding the forthcoming Fusaka upgrade. This rise is supported by the general increase in major altcoins like SOL and BNB, pushing the total cryptocurrency market capitalization to approximately $3.2 trillion.

Timothy Misir, Head of Research at BRN, noted that the recent price surge is partly due to the liquidation of significant BTC short positions above $93,000, which triggered a wave of forced buying. Additionally, spot ETF fund flows have contributed positively, with the U.S. Bitcoin spot ETF recording a net inflow of about $58.5 million, marking the fifth consecutive day of positive inflows.

data.title: DeAgentAI Reports Unauthorized Access to Discord Server
data.description: According to BlockBeats, DeAgentAI, an AI infrastructure within the Sui ecosystem, announced that its Discord server may have been compromised by hackers, leading to unauthorized access. The company has ensured that on-chain data remains secure and has promptly migrated to a new Discord server. DeAgentAI emphasized that it will not send private messages asking users to link wallets or sign any transactions.

data.title: Monero Approaches Critical $350–$360 Zone – Here is why this dip may become a buying opportunity.
data.description:
Monero, a well-known cryptocurrency, is currently nearing the crucial price range of $350 to $360. This level is seen as significant by many investors and analysts alike. As the digital asset experiences a temporary dip in its value, there are compelling reasons to consider this moment as a potential buying opportunity. Factors such as market trends, historical performance, and the unique privacy features that Monero offers may drive its value back up, making this dip an advantageous time to invest for those looking to enter or enhance their positions in this cryptocurrency.
source: https://blocknews.com/monero-approaches-critical-350-360-zone-here-is-why-this-dip-may-become-a-buying-opportunity/

data.title: Australian Bitcoin Association Challenges ABC News Report on Cryptocurrency
data.description: According to Odaily, the Australian Bitcoin Industry Association has lodged a formal complaint against an article published by ABC News. The association claims the article contains 'multiple factual errors' and 'biased reporting.' The contentious piece from ABC News asserts that Bitcoin 'lacks any practical use' and is predominantly utilized by 'those operating in the shadows.' The association is urging ABC News to issue a correction statement and to incorporate expert opinions from the cryptocurrency sector in future reports.

data.title: Gensler separates Bitcoin from the pack, calls most crypto ‘highly speculative’
data.description:
In a new Bloomberg interview, former SEC Chair Gary Gensler reiterated that Bitcoin stands apart from thousands of other crypto tokens, which he describes as ‘highly speculative’ assets.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ex-sec-gensler-cryptocurrencies-speculative?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3F_nocache%3D1764762605593%26r%3Dldfwel&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
